calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-06-2023 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of An Accounting For The Period Of 12/18/2018 Through 12/31/2020
Off Calendar Sep-13-2023 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of An Accounting For The Period Of 12/18/2018 Through 12/31/2020
Off Calendar Jun-21-2023 Continued To Sep-13-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Robert Jackson appearing via zoom for and with Jose Corea (petitioner).
John Mangini appearing via zoom for Humberto Corea Jr. (executor).
Mark Ruiz appearing via zoom for Humberto Corea Jr. (individual).

Mr. Mangini stated that they are drawing up loan documents for funding by next week, that then they will put together a final distribution petition and that parties will waive accounting. Mr. Jackson stated that his client will also waive accounting. The Status of filing accounting for the period of 12/18/18 - 12/31/20 is continued to September 13, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Petition to be filed by September 01, 2023.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-26-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances; John Magini appearing via zoom for Humberto Corea Jr. (Executor).
Jose Corea (Petitioner) in propria persona appearing via zoom.

Attorney Robert Jackson was unable to attend the hearing today. Mr. Mangini stated they went to mediation, that they settled the matter and that the settlement agreement is signed. Mr. Mangini spoke of the terms of the settlement agreement, stated that Humberto will pay Jose $440,000.00 in exchange for his interest in the estate, that Humberto is trying to obtain a loan to pay Jose and that this to be done no later than June 30, 2023. Mr. Mangini also stated that if they don't meet the deadline, the property will need to be listed for sale. The Court stated that it sees so many problems with this, that it will not allow distribution that is inconsistent with the testamentary instrument or the laws of succession unless they get the settlement approved, and that its understanding is that the parties will not be petitioning for approval of the settlement. The Court noted that the accounting is inadequate. Discussion was held regarding the possibility of getting a waiver of accounting. The current accounting petition is off calendar. Status on filing of an accounting for the period of 12/18/18 through 12/31/2020 is set for June 21, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court encouraged the parties to continue to work together. Mr. Mangini's petition can be given a hearing date of June 21, 2023.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Ateesh Datt; Deputy: Maloney; Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Continued To Apr-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Mangini appeared by zoom for Humberto Corea, Jr. (Executor).
Jose Corea appeard by zoom in propria persona.

Mr. Mangini stated that a written agreement is being negotiated right now, that all of the terms have been orally agreed to at this point, that they would like a date in April for filing of a petition for final distribution and that the agreement is conditioned upon Jose Corea receiving his distribution no later than by June 30, 2023. Mr. Mangini also stated they will not be asking the court for approval of the settlement agreement, and that they will do it themselves, but they need to be able to meet the deadline. Status of mediation is off calendar. The First Account and Report of Executor, etc. is continued to April 26, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition for final distribution is to be given a hearing date of April 26, 2023 as approved by the Judicial Officer.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-07-2022 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Jackson, via Zoom, for Jose Corea, Objector; John Mangini, via Zoom, for Humberto Correa, Petitioner, and Mark Ruiz, via Zoom, for Humberto Correa, individually.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the accounting petition and status of sale of the Estate's real property. The court queries counsel regarding whether they have considered mediation with counsel reporting that they have not. The court offers counsel the option of an order to pro bono mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than Feb-15-2023. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Feb-22-2023. The Parties are to return on Mar-01-2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The subject matter is continued to the same date. Counsel is instructed to monitor the court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the mediation order.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Proof Of Bond
Off Calendar Nov-21-2022 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Oct-26-2022 Continued To Dec-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Jackson, via Zoom, for and with Jose Corea, Objector; John Mangini, via Zoom, for Humberto Corea, Executor.

The court queries Mr. Mangini regarding the status of a proposed order. Mr. Mangini reports that an illness has caused the delays in filing a response to Objector's opposition and clearing the Examiner's notes and requests a continuance of thirty (30) days. The court advises that it is ordering the Executor to post a bond in the amount of $550,000. The court further advises that it will consider removing the Executor if the pending issues in this matter have not been addressed by the next hearing.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Dec-07-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status of Filing Proof of Bond is set for Nov-21-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Maloney. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-31-2022 Continued To Oct-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Jun-13-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Jackson, for and with Jose Corea, Objector; John Mangini, via Zoom, for Humberto Corea, Personal Representative.

Mr. Mangini reports that his client provided a counteroffer and is in the process of obtaining a loan and has hired a real estate agent and requests a continuance of forty-five (45) days to prepare and submit a formal offer. Mr. Jackson reports that the counteroffer Mr. Mangini provided is not an actual offer but an intent to offer his client a buy-out. Mr. Jackson points to the constant delays and that this issue is at a standstill. The court advises Mr. Mangini that the property needs to be sold to close this estate and that his client will need to sell the property or submit a concrete counteroffer.

The Status on Sale of Real Property is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Closing Of The Estate
Off Calendar May-09-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar May-09-2022 Continued To Jun-13-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Jackson, via Zoom, for Jose Corea, Petitioner; John Mangini, via Zoom, for Humberto Corea, Objector.

Mr. Mangini reports of advising his client to seek separate counsel or to consult with a realtor regarding purchasing Jose Corea's percentage of the estate's real property to avoid a possible conflict of interest. Mr. Mangini reports that his client advised him that he had sent a proposal to Mr. Jackson regarding the buy-out. Mr. Jackson confirms receipt of a proposal letter from Humberto but that it lacked concrete steps to secure a buy-out. The court points to the previous calling of this matter where it asked for a status regarding sale of the property by today's calling of the case. Mr. Mangini reports that he believed that his client would have a formal proposal ready by now.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Jun-13-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court advises Mr. Jackson to further review the proposal sent to him and to discuss a possible counter offer with his client.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Closing Of The Estate
Off Calendar Apr-11-2022 Continued To May-09-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Jackson, via Zoom, for Jose Corea, Objector; John Mangini, via CourtCall, for Humberto Corea, Personal Representative.

The court points counsel to the lack of progress in this matter since January of this year. Mr. Mangini reports that he has received information from his client and that he should have an accounting ready to file, shortly. Mr. Mangini also advises that his client's intention was to obtain a loan to buy-out his brother's share of the real property and remain there, but his client was unable to secure a loan and accordingly, Jose Corea would like to sell the real property and equally distribute the proceeds. Mr. Mangini believes that his client would be amenable to selling the real property if the court instructed him to do so. Mr. Jackson offers testimony regarding his position and reports that Mr. Mangini had advised him that he would send him a proposal but that he never received one.

After additional discussion, the Status on Close of Estate is continued to May-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on Sale of Real Property is set for the same date. The court instructs Mr. Mangini to provide Mr. Jackson with an update regarding the sale of the real property by May-02-2022.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Closing Of The Estate
Off Calendar Feb-28-2022 Continued To Apr-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Jackson, via ZOOM, for Jose Corea, Objector; John Mangini, via ZOOM, for Humberto Corea, Executor.

Mr. Mangini confirms that his client resides in the estate's real property and reports that a proposal is being considered which he plans to forward to Mr. Jackson in hopes of reaching an agreement. Mr. Jackson confirms that he has yet to receive anything from Mr. Mangini confirms his clients' interest in the estate and that his client and other beneficiaries have been waiting for distributions. Mr. Mangini advises that he intends to have his proposal to Mr. Jackson by the end of this week. The court instructs Mr. Mangini to forward his proposal to Mr. Jackson by Mar-11-2022.

The Status of Closing of Estate is continued to Apr-11-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ronald Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-03-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Jackson, Esq., counsel for Jose Corea, Petitioner (appearing by video conference); John Mangini, Esq., counsel for Humberto Corea, Petitioner (appearing by telephone).

The Court notes the filing of competing petitions. Counsel advise the Court of the essential nature of the conflict and attempts thus far to address outstanding issues. Humberto Corea would prefer to buy his brother Jose Corea's interest in the real property that comprises the corpus of the estate (as he is the current resident of the property) but in the absence of that option, will seek to sell the property.

After discussion, the Court takes the matter under submission but has the intention to grant the petition of Humberto Corea and will set a status hearing for closing the estate for February 28, 2022 at 9:00 a.m.

The Court admits into probate the will dated September 27, 2016.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Continued To Mar-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Jackson for Petitioner.

The Court noted that there is no response to the Probate Examiner's notes. Mr. Jackson stated that they have been able to account for most of what is in the Probate Examiner's notes, that the will has apparently been marked up, that the will was received from the Sister as a result of a law suit, and that he contacted the Attorney, but the Attorney has no information. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to March 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
